title: Linked List Cycle
problem_statement: Detect whether a linked list has a cycle in it.

examples.explanation: The tail connects to the second node, forming a cycle.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4]
intuition: Use fast and slow pointers to detect a cycle without extra memory.

approaches.name: Floyd’s cycle detection
approaches.description: Move one pointer by one step and another by two steps; if they meet, a cycle exists.
approaches.why_not: Hash set detection uses O(n) space, while two pointers are more efficient.

interview_qa.q: Why does the fast pointer help?
interview_qa.a: It closes the gap when a cycle exists, eventually meeting the slow pointer.
O(1), because no extra data structures are needed.
